commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5fec74c25d2cbff81671bba4d7a69cfb5de13868)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared assistant-visible-text regex for line-start `commentary to=...` scaffolding and four sanitizer unit tests for stripping or preserving those lines.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +45. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main WebChat reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection plus the issue's screenshot/context gives a medium-confidence path through unsanitized `chat.history` and non-phase stream projections.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are narrow and mechanical: complete the WebChat display-boundary coverage, align the regex guard, and add projection-path tests/proof.

target.title: fix(text): strip commentary to=functions.* trace lines from assistant output (fixes #89668)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?
Strips Anthropic-style `commentary to=functions.*` internal protocol lines from assistant output, preventing them from leaking into user-visible WebChat replies.

## Related Issue
Fixes #89668

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made
- `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`: Added `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` regex and integrated it into `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` to catch `commentary to=functions.browser` and similar internal protocol lines.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Internal tool-call commentary text (e.g. `commentary to=functions.browser`) was rendered into user-visible WebChat chat bubbles, making assistant replies look like they contained spam or broken output.
- **Environment tested:** Node.js on macOS
- **Steps run after the patch:** Verified the new regex `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` against representative commentary lines and confirmed `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` includes it in its filter chain.
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n 'commentary' src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts
16:  /(?:📊|🛠️|📖|📝|🔍|🔎|⚙️|tool[-_ ]?call|tool[-_ ]?result|function[-_ ]?call|commentary\s+to=)/i;
26:  /^(?:>\s*)?commentary\s+to\s*=\s*[\w.]+\b/i;
```
```
$ node -e "const re=/^(?:>\s*)?commentary\s+to\s*=\s*[\w.]+\b/i; console.log('commentary to=functions.browser:', re.test('commentary to=functions.browser_tool_use:')); console.log('commentary to=server_tool:', re.test('commentary to=server_tool.input_delta')); console.log('normal text:', re.test('Here is some normal text'));"
commentary to=functions.browser: true
commentary to=server_tool: true
normal text: false
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Lines matching `commentary to=functions.*` pattern are now detected by `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` and stripped by `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` in all sanitizer profiles. Normal text remains unaffected.
- **What was not tested:** Live browser environment

## Checklist
- [x] Read Contributing Guide
- [x] Conventional Commits
- [x] Searched for duplicates
- [x] Only related changes
- [x] Code compiles and runs
- [x] Added verification
- [x] Tested on macOS
